# UI States Reference

Use this reference when building pages, forms, async views, or mutation flows.
A feature is not complete when only the happy path renders.

---

## Core Principle

Every piece of UI that depends on data or user action has multiple possible states.
Identify and implement all relevant states before considering a task done.

---

## Async Data States

Any view that loads data should handle these states:

### Loading

Show meaningful loading UI. Prefer skeletons over spinners when the layout is predictable.

```tsx
// Skeleton — preferred for known layouts
function UserListSkeleton() {
  return (
    <div aria-busy="true" aria-label="Loading users">
      {Array.from({ length: 5 }, (_, i) => (
        <div key={i} className="skeleton-row">
          <div className="skeleton skeleton-avatar" />
          <div className="skeleton skeleton-text" />
        </div>
      ))}
    </div>
  );
}

// Spinner — for unknown layouts or small areas
function LoadingSpinner({ label = "Loading" }: { label?: string }) {
  return (
    <div role="status" aria-label={label}>
      <Spinner aria-hidden="true" />
      <span className="sr-only">{label}</span>
    </div>
  );
}
```

**When to use which:**
| Scenario | UI |
|---|---|
| Page or section with known layout | Skeleton |
| Button action in progress | Spinner inside button or disabled + loading text |
| Unknown content shape | Centered spinner |
| Inline loading (e.g., search results) | Skeleton or subtle indicator |

### Empty

Empty state should explain what the user can do, not just say "nothing here."

```tsx
function EmptyState({
  icon,
  title,
  description,
  action,
}: {
  icon?: React.ReactNode;
  title: string;
  description: string;
  action?: { label: string; onClick: () => void };
}) {
  return (
    <div className="empty-state" role="status">
      {icon && <div aria-hidden="true">{icon}</div>}
      <h3>{title}</h3>
      <p>{description}</p>
      {action && <button onClick={action.onClick}>{action.label}</button>}
    </div>
  );
}

// Usage
<EmptyState
  icon={<InboxIcon />}
  title="No messages yet"
  description="When someone sends you a message, it will appear here."
  action={{ label: "Start a conversation", onClick: openCompose }}
/>
```

### Error

Error states must be actionable. Tell the user what happened and what they can do.

```tsx
function ErrorState({
  error,
  onRetry,
}: {
  error: string;
  onRetry?: () => void;
}) {
  return (
    <div role="alert" className="error-state">
      <AlertIcon aria-hidden="true" />
      <p>{error}</p>
      {onRetry && <button onClick={onRetry}>Try again</button>}
    </div>
  );
}
```

### Success (Post-Mutation)

After a successful mutation, confirm the action to the user.

```tsx
// Inline confirmation
{state.status === "submitted" && (
  <p role="status" className="success-message">
    Changes saved successfully.
  </p>
)}

// Toast notification (when the user has moved on)
toast.success("User invited successfully");
```

---

## Combining States in a Component

```tsx
// React
type AsyncState<T> =
  | { status: "idle" }
  | { status: "loading" }
  | { status: "error"; error: string }
  | { status: "success"; data: T };

function UserList() {
  const [state, setState] = React.useState<AsyncState<User[]>>({ status: "idle" });

  switch (state.status) {
    case "idle":
    case "loading":
      return <UserListSkeleton />;
    case "error":
      return <ErrorState error={state.error} onRetry={refetch} />;
    case "success":
      if (state.data.length === 0) {
        return <EmptyState title="No users" description="Invite your team." />;
      }
      return (
        <ul role="list">
          {state.data.map((user) => (
            <li key={user.id}><UserCard user={user} /></li>
          ))}
        </ul>
      );
  }
}
```

```vue
<!-- Vue (Nuxt) — useFetch handles states for you -->
<script setup lang="ts">
const { data: users, status, error, refresh } = await useFetch<User[]>("/api/users");
</script>

<template>
  <UserListSkeleton v-if="status === 'pending'" />

  <div v-else-if="error" role="alert">
    <p>{{ error.message }}</p>
    <button @click="refresh()">Try again</button>
  </div>

  <EmptyState
    v-else-if="!users?.length"
    title="No users"
    description="Invite your team to get started."
  />

  <ul v-else role="list">
    <li v-for="user in users" :key="user.id">
      <UserCard :user="user" />
    </li>
  </ul>
</template>
```

---

## Form States

### Field-Level States

| State | Visual | Accessibility |
|---|---|---|
| Default | Normal border | — |
| Focused | Focus ring | `:focus-visible` |
| Filled | May show checkmark | — |
| Error | Red border + error text | `aria-invalid="true"` + `aria-describedby` |
| Disabled | Muted appearance | `disabled` attribute |

### Form-Level States

```tsx
function ContactForm() {
  const [state, action, isPending] = useActionState(submitContact, {
    status: "idle" as const,
  });

  return (
    <form action={action}>
      {/* Fields */}

      {state.status === "error" && (
        <div role="alert" className="form-error">
          {state.message}
        </div>
      )}

      {state.status === "success" && (
        <div role="status" className="form-success">
          Thank you! We'll be in touch.
        </div>
      )}

      <button type="submit" disabled={isPending}>
        {isPending ? "Sending…" : "Send Message"}
      </button>
    </form>
  );
}
```

### Validation Timing

| Timing | When |
|---|---|
| On submit | Always — this is the baseline |
| On blur | Good for fields with specific formats (email, URL) |
| On change (debounced) | Good for search, username availability |
| Real-time | Only for character counts or simple format hints |

---

## Button States

Buttons carry more states than most developers implement.

```tsx
interface ActionButtonProps {
  onClick: () => Promise<void>;
  children: React.ReactNode;
}

function ActionButton({ onClick, children }: ActionButtonProps) {
  const [state, setState] = React.useState<
    "idle" | "loading" | "success" | "error"
  >("idle");

  async function handleClick() {
    setState("loading");
    try {
      await onClick();
      setState("success");
      // Reset after feedback
      setTimeout(() => setState("idle"), 2000);
    } catch {
      setState("error");
      setTimeout(() => setState("idle"), 3000);
    }
  }

  return (
    <button
      onClick={handleClick}
      disabled={state === "loading"}
      aria-busy={state === "loading"}
    >
      {state === "idle" && children}
      {state === "loading" && "Processing…"}
      {state === "success" && "Done!"}
      {state === "error" && "Failed — try again"}
    </button>
  );
}
```

---

## Pagination and Infinite Scroll

```tsx
// Pagination states
function PaginatedList({ items, totalPages, currentPage, onPageChange }) {
  // No results at all
  if (totalPages === 0) {
    return <EmptyState title="No results" description="Try a different search." />;
  }

  return (
    <>
      <ul role="list">
        {items.map((item) => (
          <li key={item.id}><ItemCard item={item} /></li>
        ))}
      </ul>

      <nav aria-label="Pagination">
        <button
          onClick={() => onPageChange(currentPage - 1)}
          disabled={currentPage === 1}
        >
          Previous
        </button>
        <span aria-current="page">
          Page {currentPage} of {totalPages}
        </span>
        <button
          onClick={() => onPageChange(currentPage + 1)}
          disabled={currentPage === totalPages}
        >
          Next
        </button>
      </nav>
    </>
  );
}
```

---

## Optimistic Updates

Show the expected result immediately, then reconcile with the server.

```tsx
// React
function ToggleFavorite({ item }: { item: Item }) {
  const [optimisticFavorite, setOptimisticFavorite] = React.useState(item.isFavorite);

  async function toggle() {
    const newValue = !optimisticFavorite;
    setOptimisticFavorite(newValue);
    try {
      await updateFavorite(item.id, newValue);
    } catch {
      setOptimisticFavorite(!newValue);
      toast.error("Failed to update favorite");
    }
  }

  return (
    <button onClick={toggle} aria-pressed={optimisticFavorite}
      aria-label={optimisticFavorite ? "Remove from favorites" : "Add to favorites"}>
      {optimisticFavorite ? <HeartFilledIcon /> : <HeartIcon />}
    </button>
  );
}
```

```vue
<!-- Vue -->
<script setup lang="ts">
const props = defineProps<{ item: Item }>();

const optimisticFavorite = ref(props.item.isFavorite);

async function toggle() {
  const newValue = !optimisticFavorite.value;
  optimisticFavorite.value = newValue;
  try {
    await $fetch(`/api/favorites/${props.item.id}`, {
      method: "PUT",
      body: { isFavorite: newValue },
    });
  } catch {
    optimisticFavorite.value = !newValue;
    toast.error("Failed to update favorite");
  }
}
</script>

<template>
  <button
    @click="toggle"
    :aria-pressed="optimisticFavorite"
    :aria-label="optimisticFavorite ? 'Remove from favorites' : 'Add to favorites'"
  >
    <HeartFilledIcon v-if="optimisticFavorite" />
    <HeartIcon v-else />
  </button>
</template>
```

---

## Offline and Connectivity States

For apps that should handle network interruptions.

```tsx
function useOnlineStatus(): boolean {
  const [isOnline, setIsOnline] = React.useState(
    typeof navigator !== "undefined" ? navigator.onLine : true
  );

  React.useEffect(() => {
    const handleOnline = () => setIsOnline(true);
    const handleOffline = () => setIsOnline(false);
    window.addEventListener("online", handleOnline);
    window.addEventListener("offline", handleOffline);
    return () => {
      window.removeEventListener("online", handleOnline);
      window.removeEventListener("offline", handleOffline);
    };
  }, []);

  return isOnline;
}

// Banner component
function OfflineBanner() {
  const isOnline = useOnlineStatus();
  if (isOnline) return null;

  return (
    <div role="alert" className="offline-banner">
      <WifiOffIcon aria-hidden="true" />
      You're offline. Changes will sync when you reconnect.
    </div>
  );
}
```

---

## Permission and Authorization States

Not all users can see or do everything. Handle forbidden and unauthorized states explicitly.

```tsx
type PermissionState =
  | { status: "authorized" }
  | { status: "unauthorized"; reason: "not_logged_in" }
  | { status: "forbidden"; reason: string };

function ProtectedSection({
  permission,
  children,
}: {
  permission: PermissionState;
  children: React.ReactNode;
}) {
  switch (permission.status) {
    case "unauthorized":
      return (
        <div role="status">
          <p>Please sign in to access this section.</p>
          <a href="/login">Sign in</a>
        </div>
      );
    case "forbidden":
      return (
        <div role="status">
          <p>You don't have access to this section.</p>
          <p className="hint">{permission.reason}</p>
        </div>
      );
    case "authorized":
      return <>{children}</>;
  }
}
```

---

## Stale-While-Revalidate Pattern

Show stale data while fetching fresh data. This prevents unnecessary loading states on refetch.

```tsx
function UserDashboard() {
  const { data, isRefetching, dataUpdatedAt } = useQuery({
    queryKey: ["dashboard"],
    queryFn: fetchDashboard,
    staleTime: 30_000, // Consider fresh for 30s
  });

  return (
    <section aria-busy={isRefetching}>
      {isRefetching && (
        <p aria-live="polite" className="sr-only">Refreshing data…</p>
      )}
      {data && (
        <>
          <DashboardContent data={data} />
          <p className="hint">
            Last updated: <time dateTime={new Date(dataUpdatedAt).toISOString()}>
              {formatRelative(dataUpdatedAt)}
            </time>
          </p>
        </>
      )}
    </section>
  );
}
```

Reduce opacity or show a subtle indicator — never replace visible data with a full loading state on refetch.

---

## Confirmation Patterns

Destructive or irreversible actions need explicit confirmation.

```tsx
function DeleteButton({ onConfirm, itemName }: { onConfirm: () => void; itemName: string }) {
  const [isConfirming, setIsConfirming] = React.useState(false);
  const timerRef = React.useRef<ReturnType<typeof setTimeout>>();

  function handleFirstClick() {
    setIsConfirming(true);
    // Auto-reset after 3 seconds if no confirmation
    timerRef.current = setTimeout(() => setIsConfirming(false), 3000);
  }

  function handleConfirm() {
    clearTimeout(timerRef.current);
    setIsConfirming(false);
    onConfirm();
  }

  React.useEffect(() => () => clearTimeout(timerRef.current), []);

  if (isConfirming) {
    return (
      <button onClick={handleConfirm} className="confirm-delete" aria-live="polite">
        Click again to delete {itemName}
      </button>
    );
  }

  return (
    <button onClick={handleFirstClick}>
      Delete {itemName}
    </button>
  );
}
```

Alternative patterns:
- **Dialog confirmation** — for high-impact actions (account deletion, publishing).
- **Inline confirm** — for medium-impact actions (deleting a list item).
- **Undo toast** — for low-impact actions that are easily reversible (archiving, moving).

---

## Multi-Step Flow States

Wizards, onboarding, and checkout flows need step management.

```tsx
type Step = "account" | "profile" | "confirm";

interface WizardState {
  currentStep: Step;
  completedSteps: Set<Step>;
  data: Partial<WizardData>;
}

function StepIndicator({
  steps,
  currentStep,
  completedSteps,
}: {
  steps: Step[];
  currentStep: Step;
  completedSteps: Set<Step>;
}) {
  return (
    <nav aria-label="Progress">
      <ol>
        {steps.map((step, index) => {
          const isCurrent = step === currentStep;
          const isComplete = completedSteps.has(step);

          return (
            <li
              key={step}
              aria-current={isCurrent ? "step" : undefined}
            >
              <span className="sr-only">
                {isComplete ? "Completed: " : isCurrent ? "Current: " : ""}
              </span>
              Step {index + 1}: {step}
            </li>
          );
        })}
      </ol>
    </nav>
  );
}
```

Rules:
- Show which step the user is on and how many remain.
- Allow going back without losing data.
- Validate each step before allowing progression.
- Use `aria-current="step"` on the current step.

---

## Timeout and Long-Running Operations

```tsx
function LongRunningAction({ onStart }: { onStart: () => Promise<void> }) {
  const [state, setState] = React.useState<
    "idle" | "processing" | "slow" | "success" | "error"
  >("idle");

  async function handleStart() {
    setState("processing");

    // If it takes more than 3s, show a "still working" message
    const slowTimer = setTimeout(() => setState("slow"), 3000);

    try {
      await onStart();
      setState("success");
    } catch {
      setState("error");
    } finally {
      clearTimeout(slowTimer);
    }
  }

  return (
    <div>
      <button onClick={handleStart} disabled={state === "processing" || state === "slow"}>
        {state === "idle" && "Generate Report"}
        {state === "processing" && "Generating…"}
        {state === "slow" && "Still working — this may take a moment…"}
        {state === "success" && "Done!"}
        {state === "error" && "Failed — try again"}
      </button>
      {state === "slow" && (
        <p role="status" aria-live="polite">
          This is taking longer than expected. Please wait.
        </p>
      )}
    </div>
  );
}
```

---

## State Checklist

Before finishing any async or interactive UI, confirm:

- [ ] **Loading** — Is there a loading indicator? Does it match the layout?
- [ ] **Empty** — What does the user see when there's no data? Is there a call to action?
- [ ] **Error** — Can the user understand what went wrong? Can they retry?
- [ ] **Success** — Does the user get confirmation that their action worked?
- [ ] **Disabled** — Is the reason clear? Is it accessible?
- [ ] **Pending/Submitting** — Is the button disabled? Is there visual feedback?
- [ ] **Partial failure** — If one part fails, does the rest still work?
- [ ] **Stale data** — Can the user refresh or is data auto-refreshed?
- [ ] **Offline** — What happens when network drops? Is there a banner or degraded mode?
- [ ] **Permission** — What does the user see if they lack access? Is there a clear next step?
- [ ] **Confirmation** — Do destructive actions require confirmation? Is undo available where appropriate?
- [ ] **Long-running** — Is there feedback if an operation takes longer than expected?
- [ ] **Multi-step** — Can the user track progress, go back, and resume without data loss?
